content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms from general agent research can be repurposed to fix the 'code blindness' flaw of modern coding agents, which get stuck repeating trivial code edits by only optimizing for a given task specification instead of exploring alternative, implicitly better solutions. This creates a new class of 'curious coding agents' that actively explore under-tested edge cases in codebases on their own to reduce post-deployment bugs.
